data.title: Crypto Startups Secure Nearly $3 Billion in Funding This Year
data.description: Crypto startups have successfully raised close to $3 billion in funding this year. According to NS3.AI, the third week of March alone saw $155 million in investments. Among the notable funding rounds, MetaComp secured $35 million in a pre-Series A round, Ironlight raised $21 million in a Series A round, and TransFi obtained $19.2 million. These investments highlight the continued interest and confidence in the crypto sector despite market fluctuations.

data.title: Pentagon Confirms Emergency Landing of U.S. F-35A in Middle East
data.description:
The Pentagon has confirmed that a U.S. F-35A fighter jet, conducting a mission over Iran, made an emergency return and landed at an undisclosed U.S. military base in the Middle East. According to BlockBeats, the incident was reported by the American website 'War Zone.'

The Iranian Revolutionary Guard released a video showing the stealth fighter jet being attacked. Foreign media suggest this may be the first instance of an F-35 being hit in a combat environment, marking a new world record in aerial combat for Iran.

data.title: Prediction Market Platform Valuations Surpass Traditional Sports Betting Companies
Hong Kong Police Uncover Cryptocurrency Fraud Targeting Retiree
data.description: Hong Kong police have revealed a cryptocurrency scam involving a 66-year-old retiree. According to Odaily, the victim fell prey to fraudulent schemes by so-called 'cryptocurrency investment experts' starting in September 2025. Initially, the retiree lost HKD 1.4 million, and in an attempt to recover the losses, paid another HKD 600,000 as a guarantee to a different 'expert.' Subsequently, the victim was persuaded to purchase HKD 4.6 million worth of cryptocurrency and transfer it to a specified account. After receiving the funds, the scammers disappeared, resulting in a total loss of HKD 6.6 million over six months. The victim eventually sought police assistance. Authorities caution against trusting 'guaranteed' investment opportunities and services claiming to recover losses, as they may lead to further scams.

data.title: RDNT rose above 0.005 USDT, with a 24-hour increase of 33.18%.
data.description: Market data shows that RDNT has risen above 0.005 USDT, currently trading at 0.00562 USDT, a 24-hour increase of 33.18%. Previously, Binance announced that it would add RDNT to its watchlist, indicating a potential risk of delisting.

data.title: German Chancellor Merz Reaffirms Distance from Trump’s Political Base
data.description: German Chancellor Friedrich Merz has reiterated his stance of distancing himself from the political base of U.S. President Donald Trump. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting Merz's intention to maintain diplomatic engagement with Trump despite the ideological differences. Merz's approach reflects a commitment to fostering international relations while navigating the complexities of differing political ideologies.

data.title: Phantom Receives No-Action Relief from CFTC for Derivatives Interface
data.description: Phantom has been granted no-action relief by the Commodity Futures Trading Commission's (CFTC) Market Participants Division as of March 17. According to NS3.AI, this decision allows Phantom's wallet to interact with Commission-regulated derivatives without the need to register as an introducing broker. However, registered futures intermediaries and markets are required to maintain customer relationships, custody, and clearing functions. The relief is at the staff level and may be subject to modification or termination. Phantom clarified that the relief does not extend to decentralized finance (DeFi) derivatives or tokenized prediction markets.

data.title: SEC Considers Optional Quarterly Reporting for U.S. Public Companies
data.description: The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) is reportedly working on a proposal that could allow public companies in the United States to opt for semiannual financial updates instead of the current quarterly reporting system. According to NS3.AI, this potential change would maintain the requirement for annual reports and event-driven disclosures, but it might result in fewer standardized checkpoints for investors between yearly filings. This move is seen as an effort to reduce the regulatory burden on companies while still ensuring transparency and accountability in financial reporting.

data.title: Iranian Attacks on U.S. Bases Cause $800 Million in Damages, BBC Reports
data.description: Recent analysis indicates that Iranian attacks on U.S. bases have resulted in $800 million in damages. According to Jin10, the report highlights the significant financial impact of these assaults. The analysis underscores the ongoing tensions between Iran and the United States, with military engagements contributing to substantial economic costs. The report did not specify the exact locations or dates of the attacks, but the financial implications are clear. This development adds another layer of complexity to the geopolitical landscape, as both nations navigate their strained relations.

data.title: Two Women Convicted in $9.4 Million Amazon Fraud Scheme
data.description: Two women have been found guilty of orchestrating a fraudulent scheme that defrauded Amazon of nearly $9.4 million. According to NS3.AI, the scheme involved submitting fake vendor invoices to the company. The ill-gotten gains were reportedly used to purchase luxury vehicles and a $1 million home. One of the defendants received her sentence in 2023, while the other is scheduled to be sentenced on June 16, 2026.

data.title: Drones Repelled Near Oil Refineries in Russia's Bashkortostan Region
data.description: Authorities in Russia's Bashkortostan region reported that drones were repelled overnight near major oil refineries. Bloomberg posted on X that these refineries had been targeted multiple times last year. The incident highlights ongoing security concerns in the area, which is crucial for Russia's oil production. No further details were provided about the drones or the measures taken to counter them.

data.title: Iran Issues Highest Denomination Banknote Amid Inflation Concerns
data.description: Tehran has introduced a new banknote with a denomination of 10 million rials, equivalent to approximately $7, marking the highest denomination in the country's history. According to Jin10, the Iranian authorities aim to curb soaring inflation and address cash demand during the ongoing conflict involving the U.S. and Israel. This week, long queues have formed at ATMs across Iran due to fears of potential electronic system failures, prompting banks to distribute the new banknotes.

data.title: Robinhood CEO: Urges Congress to Allow Stablecoin Yields
data.description: Robinhood CEO Vlad Tenev urged the U.S. Congress to allow stablecoin yields, stating that consumers should receive returns on their stablecoin holdings in the same way they would on a savings account. (Cointelegraph)

data.title: Iranian Media Reports: Israel Strikes Natanz Uranium Enrichment Facility
data.description: Israel has reportedly launched another attack on Iran's Natanz uranium enrichment facility, according to Iranian media. The facility has not reported any leakage of radioactive materials following the incident. The attack marks a continuation of tensions between the two nations, with previous incidents involving similar targets. According to Jin10, the Natanz facility is a key site in Iran's nuclear program, which has been a point of contention in international relations. The Iranian government has yet to release an official statement regarding the attack or its implications for ongoing diplomatic efforts.

data.title: Global Economy Faces Challenges Amid Varied Economic Indicators
data.description:
This week, the global economy experienced a mix of challenges and developments, as highlighted by various economic indicators. Bloomberg posted on X, noting that inflation rates in several major economies showed signs of easing, yet concerns about potential recessions persist.

In the United States, recent data indicated a slowdown in consumer spending, raising questions about the strength of the economic recovery. Meanwhile, the European Central Bank maintained its interest rates, signaling caution amid ongoing economic uncertainties in the Eurozone.

China's economic growth continues to be a focal point, with recent figures showing a slight improvement in manufacturing output. However, analysts remain cautious about the sustainability of this growth given the ongoing trade tensions and domestic challenges.

In the commodities market, oil prices fluctuated due to geopolitical tensions and supply chain disruptions, impacting global trade dynamics. Additionally, the cryptocurrency market saw volatility, with Bitcoin experiencing significant price swings throughout the week.

Overall, the global economic landscape remains complex, with various factors influencing market stability and growth prospects. Policymakers and investors are closely monitoring these developments to navigate the uncertain economic environment.

data.title: Iran's Revolutionary Guards: No Immediate Risk to Residents Near Natanz Uranium Enrichment Facility After Attack
data.description: Iran's Revolutionary Guards have reported that there is no immediate risk to residents living near the Natanz uranium enrichment facility following a recent attack. According to Jin10, the incident has raised concerns about the safety and security of the area, but officials have assured the public that measures are in place to protect local communities. The attack on the facility, which plays a crucial role in Iran's nuclear program, has drawn international attention and heightened tensions in the region. Authorities are currently investigating the circumstances surrounding the attack and are working to ensure the continued safety of the facility and its surroundings.

data.title: Iranian armed forces claim to be conducting major operations in the Strait of Hormuz.
data.description: A spokesperson for the central headquarters of the Iranian Armed Forces in Hatem-Anbia stated on the 21st local time that Iran is "taking significant action in the Strait of Hormuz." The spokesperson emphasized, "If Iran attacks its infrastructure, Iran will attack its more important and critical infrastructure." (CCTV News)

data.title: This week, US Bitcoin spot ETFs saw a net inflow of $93.1 million.
data.description: According to Farside monitoring data, US Bitcoin spot ETFs saw a net inflow of $93.1 million this week.

data.title: Austria Faces €2 Billion Budget Savings Amid Economic Concerns
data.description: Austria is required to identify up to €2 billion in budget savings as part of its fiscal consolidation efforts, according to the country's finance minister. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the growing risks to Austria's economic outlook due to the ongoing conflict in Iran. The finance minister emphasized the importance of these savings to stabilize the national budget and mitigate potential economic challenges. The situation underscores the broader impact of geopolitical tensions on European economies, necessitating careful financial planning and adjustments.
